brief_notes: Technically impressive study combining in vivo two-photon imaging with patch clamp in neonatal mice. Tables present quantitative synapse density, transmission frequency, spine/shaft ratios, domain properties, and co-activity correlations with Pearson r and p-values. The developmental trajectory from P8 to P13 is clearly documented. The 20% highly-active synapse finding and the co-activity-potentiation correlation (r=0.47) are well-supported. Sample size (11 mice, 12 dendritic areas) is modest but appropriate for this technically challenging approach.
